mission.title: minio: ecosystem: Validate all spark and hadoop supported s3 connectors
mission.description: ecosystem: Validate all spark and hadoop supported s3 connectors. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify minio troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your minio deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n minio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio -n minio --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review minio configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant minio configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n minio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio
kubectl get configmap -n minio -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=minio
```
## Expected behaviour

I want to use spark-shell (v2.0.1) to connect to minio local to read input data and write back output data.

steps.title: Apply the fix for ecosystem: Validate all spark and hadoop supported s3…
steps.description:
Make PutObject a nop for an object which ends with "/" and si
```yaml
com.amazonaws.services.s3.model.AmazonS3Exception: Status Code: 400, AWS Service: Amazon S3, AWS Request ID: null, AWS Error Code: null, AWS Error Message: Bad Request
```

steps.title: Confirm ecosystem: Validate all spark and hadoop… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio -n minio --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n minio --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Make PutObject a nop for an object which ends with "/" and si
codeSnippets: com.amazonaws.services.s3.model.AmazonS3Exception: Status Code: 400, AWS Service: Amazon S3, AWS Request ID: null, AWS Error Code: null, AWS Error Message: Bad Request

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with minio installed or the issue environment reproducible.
